seniority.level: Senior IC (likely Senior PM / Staff PM)
level_signals: Comp range $305K-$385K base, consistent with Senior or Staff IC at Anthropic
Drives strategy and roadmap across CPQ, approvals, and deal automation
Synthesizes competing stakeholder needs and communicates to senior leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Deeply understand sellers, Deal Desk, Legal, and Order Management workflows and where tooling can make them faster and more reliable.
Partner with engineering to design the systems underpinning quoting, approvals, and deal execution.
Drive product strategy and roadmap across CPQ, approvals, and deal automation.
Synthesize competing stakeholder needs across GTM, Finance, and Legal and communicate prioritization clearly to senior leadership.
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Built or scaled CPQ, quoting, or approval-workflow systems supporting complex enterprise deals.
Salesforce/CPQ and quote-to-cash knowledge, including how quoting connects to billing and provisioning.

scope.surface: GTM commercial infrastructure — CPQ, quoting and pricing configuration, approval workflows, deal automation, cloud marketplace transactions
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS / AI (revenue motion for Anthropic's commercial deals)
primary_metrics_inferred: Enterprise deal volume processed through CPQ and automation
core_responsibilities: Deeply understand sellers, Deal Desk, Legal, and Order Management workflows and where tooling can make them faster and more reliable
Partner with engineering to design the systems underpinning quoting, approvals, and deal execution

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($305K-$385K)
Visa sponsorship explicitly offered (with reasonable caveats)
Clear product surface with concrete ownership (CPQ, quoting, approvals, deal automation)
Encourages applications even from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Explicit DE&I statement and awareness of imposter syndrome
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Years of experience not specified; seniority expectations are implicit and may be difficult to assess pre-application

Most requirements are under 'Strong candidates may have' framing, making it difficult to distinguish hard vs soft requirements; the responsibilities section implies strong hard requirements but they lack explicit 'required' labeling
